questions.question: Which port does HTTP use?
questions.explanation: HTTP (HyperText Transfer Protocol) uses port 80. It uses TCP/UDP/SCTP and is the standard port for unencrypted web traffic.

questions.question: Which port does HTTPS use?
questions.explanation: HTTPS (HTTP over SSL) uses port 443. It uses TCP/UDP/SCTP and encrypts web traffic using TLS/SSL.

questions.question: Which port does SSH / SCP / SFTP use?
questions.explanation: SSH, SCP, and SFTP all use port 22 (TCP/UDP/SCTP). SSH provides encrypted remote access, SCP for secure copy, SFTP for secure file transfer.

questions.question: Which port does Telnet use?
questions.explanation: Telnet uses port 23 (TCP). It is an unencrypted remote login protocol — largely replaced by SSH due to security risks.

questions.question: Which port does SMTP use?
questions.explanation: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) uses port 25 (TCP). It is used to send email from client to server and between mail servers.

questions.question: Which port does DNS use?
questions.explanation: DNS (Domain Name System) uses port 53 (TCP/UDP). UDP is used for standard queries; TCP is used for zone transfers and large responses.

questions.question: Which port does FTP data transfer use?
questions.explanation: FTP uses port 20 (TCP/SCTP) for data transfer and port 21 for control/commands.
questions.takeaway: FTP Data = Port 20 | FTP Control = Port 21

questions.question: Which port does FTP control use?
questions.explanation: FTP control connection uses port 21 (TCP/UDP/SCTP) for sending commands like login, directory listing, and file operations.

questions.question: Which port does POP3 use?
questions.explanation: POP3 (Post Office Protocol v3) uses port 110 (TCP). It is used to retrieve email from a mail server.

questions.question: Which port does IMAP use?
questions.explanation: IMAP (Internet Message Access Protocol) uses port 143 (TCP/UDP). It allows email to be accessed and managed directly on the mail server.

questions.question: Which port does NTP use?
questions.explanation: NTP (Network Time Protocol) uses port 123 (UDP). It synchronizes clocks of devices across a network.

questions.question: Which port does SNMP agent use?
questions.explanation: SNMP agents listen on port 161 (UDP). SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol) is used to monitor and manage network devices.

questions.question: Which port does SNMP trap use?
questions.explanation: SNMP trap messages are sent to port 162 (UDP). Traps are alerts sent by devices to the SNMP manager.

questions.question: Which port does LDAP use?
questions.explanation: LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) uses port 389 (TCP/UDP). It is used to access and maintain directory services like Active Directory.

questions.question: Which port does RDP (Remote Desktop Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: RDP (Remote Desktop Protocol) uses port 3389 (TCP). It is used for remote graphical desktop access to Windows systems.

questions.question: Which port does MySQL database use?
questions.explanation: MySQL uses port 3306 (TCP/UDP). It is the default port for MySQL database connections.

questions.question: Which port does PostgreSQL use?
questions.explanation: PostgreSQL uses port 5432 (TCP). It is the default port for PostgreSQL database server connections.

questions.question: Which port does DHCP server use?
questions.explanation: DHCP server listens on port 67 (UDP). DHCP clients send requests from port 68. Together they automate IP address assignment.
questions.takeaway: DHCP Server = Port 67 | DHCP Client = Port 68 (both UDP)

questions.question: Which port does TFTP use?
questions.explanation: TFTP (Trivial File Transfer Protocol) uses port 69 (UDP). It is a simplified file transfer protocol with no authentication, often used for booting network devices.

questions.question: Which port does Kerberos authentication use?
questions.explanation: Kerberos authentication uses port 88 (TCP/UDP). It is a network authentication protocol used heavily in Microsoft Active Directory environments.

questions.question: Which port does BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) uses port 179 (TCP). It is the exterior gateway routing protocol used between autonomous systems on the internet.
questions.takeaway: BGP = Port 179 (TCP) — internet routing between AS

questions.question: Which port does SMB / Microsoft DS use?
questions.explanation: SMB (Server Message Block) / Microsoft DS uses port 445 (TCP/UDP). It is used for Windows file sharing, printers, and Active Directory.

questions.question: Which port does SMTP over TLS/SSL use?
questions.explanation: SMTP over TLS/SSL (secure email submission) uses port 465 (TCP). Port 587 is used for SMTP submission with STARTTLS.

questions.question: Which port does IMAP over SSL (IMAPS) use?
questions.explanation: IMAPS (IMAP over SSL) uses port 993 (TCP). It provides encrypted email retrieval, securing the standard IMAP port 143.

questions.question: Which port does POP3 over SSL (POP3S) use?
questions.explanation: POP3S (POP3 over SSL) uses port 995 (TCP/UDP). It secures the standard POP3 email retrieval protocol with SSL/TLS.

questions.question: Which port does FTPS (FTP over SSL) use?
questions.explanation: FTPS uses port 990 (TCP) for control and port 989 for data. It is FTP secured with TLS/SSL encryption.
questions.takeaway: FTPS Control = Port 990 | FTPS Data = Port 989 (TCP)

questions.question: Which port does OpenVPN use?
questions.explanation: OpenVPN uses port 1194 (TCP/UDP). It is an open-source VPN protocol widely used for creating secure point-to-point tunnels.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft SQL Server use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft SQL Server uses port 1433 (TCP) for database connections. Port 1434 is used for the SQL Server browser service.

questions.question: Which port does RADIUS authentication use?
questions.explanation: RADIUS authentication uses port 1812 (TCP/UDP). RADIUS accounting uses port 1813. It is used for centralized network access authentication.
questions.takeaway: RADIUS Auth = Port 1812 | RADIUS Accounting = Port 1813 (TCP/UDP)

questions.question: Which port does Redis use?
questions.explanation: Redis (in-memory data store) uses port 6379 (TCP). It is commonly used as a cache, message broker, and session store.

questions.question: Which port does LDAP over TLS/SSL use?
questions.explanation: LDAP over TLS/SSL uses port 636 (TCP/UDP). It secures LDAP directory queries with SSL/TLS encryption.

questions.question: Which port does NNTP over TLS/SSL use?
questions.explanation: NNTP over TLS/SSL uses port 563 (TCP/UDP). NNTP (Network News Transfer Protocol) is the protocol for Usenet newsgroups.

questions.question: Which port does SOCKS proxy use?
questions.explanation: SOCKS proxy uses port 1080 (TCP/UDP). SOCKS is a protocol that routes network packets between client and server through a proxy server.

questions.question: Which port does MongoDB use?
questions.explanation: MongoDB uses port 27017 (TCP/UDP). It is the default port for the MongoDB NoSQL database server.

questions.question: Which port does rsync use?
questions.explanation: rsync uses port 873 (TCP). It is used for efficient file synchronization and transfer between Unix/Linux systems.

questions.question: Which port does SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: SIP uses port 5060 (TCP/UDP) for unencrypted VoIP signaling. SIP-TLS uses port 5061 for encrypted signaling.

questions.question: Which port does IPSec/IKE (VPN) use?
questions.explanation: IPSec/IKE uses port 500 (UDP) for key exchange. Port 4500 (UDP) is used for IPSec NAT traversal when behind a NAT device.

questions.question: Which port does Syslog use?
questions.explanation: Syslog uses port 514 (UDP). It is the standard protocol for sending system log messages to a centralized log server.

questions.question: Which port does the echo service use?
questions.explanation: The echo service uses port 7 (TCP/UDP). It echoes back any data received — used for testing and diagnostics.
questions.takeaway: Echo = Port 7 (TCP/UDP) — returns whatever is sent

questions.question: Which port does VNC (remote desktop) use?
questions.explanation: VNC (Virtual Network Computing) uses port 5900 (TCP) for the primary display. Port 5800 is used for VNC over HTTP (browser access).
questions.takeaway: VNC = Port 5900 (TCP) | VNC over HTTP = Port 5800 (TCP)

questions.question: Which port does Telnet Secure (Telnets) use?
questions.explanation: Telnets (Telnet over SSL) uses port 992 (TCP/UDP). It is the encrypted version of the Telnet protocol.

questions.question: Which port does WHOIS use?
questions.explanation: WHOIS uses port 43 (TCP/UDP). It is used to query databases for information about domain names, IP addresses, and ASNs.

questions.question: Which port does TACACS use?
questions.explanation: TACACS (Terminal Access Controller Access Control System) uses port 49 (UDP). It provides authentication for network devices.

questions.question: Which port does HTTP Proxy / Alternative HTTP use?
questions.explanation: Port 8080 (TCP) is commonly used as an alternative HTTP port or for HTTP proxy servers. Many web servers and proxies listen on 8080.

questions.question: Which port does the Gopher protocol use?
questions.explanation: Gopher uses port 70 (TCP). It is an early internet protocol predating the World Wide Web, used to distribute and retrieve documents.

questions.question: Which port does the Finger protocol use?
questions.explanation: Finger uses port 79 (TCP). It is used to retrieve information about users on a remote system.

questions.question: Which port does WINS Replication use?
questions.explanation: WINS (Windows Internet Name Service) replication uses port 42 (TCP/UDP). WINS resolves NetBIOS names to IP addresses.

questions.question: Which port does the Chargen service use?
questions.explanation: Chargen (Character Generator Protocol) uses port 19 (TCP/UDP). It generates a stream of characters and is used for network testing.

questions.question: Which port does the Discard protocol use?
questions.explanation: Discard protocol uses port 9 (TCP/UDP). It discards any data it receives and is used for testing purposes.
questions.takeaway: Discard = Port 9 (TCP/UDP) — testing, discards all data

questions.question: Which port does DHCP client use?
questions.explanation: DHCP client uses port 68 (UDP) to receive IP address assignments from the DHCP server which listens on port 67.
questions.takeaway: DHCP Client = Port 68 (UDP) | DHCP Server = Port 67

questions.question: Which port does NetBIOS Name Service use?
questions.explanation: NetBIOS Name Service (NetBIOS-NS) uses port 137 (TCP/UDP). It resolves NetBIOS names to IP addresses on local networks.

questions.question: Which port does NetBIOS Datagram Service use?
questions.explanation: NetBIOS Datagram Service (NetBIOS-DGM) uses port 138 (TCP/UDP). It handles connectionless communication for NetBIOS.

questions.question: Which port does NetBIOS Session Service use?
questions.explanation: NetBIOS Session Service (NetBIOS-SSN) uses port 139 (TCP/UDP). It handles connection-oriented NetBIOS communication.

questions.question: Which port does IRC (Internet Relay Chat) use?
questions.explanation: IRC (Internet Relay Chat) uses port 194 (UDP). It is a real-time text messaging and chat protocol.

questions.question: Which port does AppleTalk use?
questions.explanation: AppleTalk uses port 201 (TCP/UDP). It is Apple's legacy networking protocol suite for connecting Apple devices.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft RPC EPMAP use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft RPC EPMAP (Endpoint Mapper) uses port 135 (TCP/UDP). It maps RPC services to ports and is essential for Windows RPC communication.

questions.question: Which port does rexec (Remote Execution) use?
questions.explanation: rexec (Remote Execution) uses port 512 (TCP). It allows execution of commands on a remote system — now largely replaced by SSH.

questions.question: Which port does rlogin (Remote Login) use?
questions.explanation: rlogin (Remote Login) uses port 513 (TCP). It allows users to log into a remote Unix system — replaced by SSH due to lack of encryption.

questions.question: Which port does LPD/LPR (Line Printer Daemon) use?
questions.explanation: LPD/LPR (Line Printer Daemon/Remote) uses port 515 (TCP). It is the standard Unix printing protocol.

questions.question: Which port does RIP (Routing Information Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: RIP (Routing Information Protocol) uses port 520 (UDP). It is a distance-vector routing protocol that uses hop count as its metric.

questions.question: Which port does RIPng (RIP for IPv6) use?
questions.explanation: RIPng (RIP next generation for IPv6) uses port 521 (UDP). It is the IPv6 extension of the RIP routing protocol.

questions.question: Which port does AFP (Apple Filing Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: AFP (Apple Filing Protocol) uses port 548 (TCP). It is the primary protocol for file services on macOS systems.

questions.question: Which port does RTSP (Real Time Streaming Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: RTSP (Real Time Streaming Protocol) uses port 554 (TCP/UDP). It controls streaming media sessions such as audio and video.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft DCOM use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft DCOM (Distributed Component Object Model) uses port 593 (TCP/UDP). It enables communication between COM objects over a network.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft Exchange use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Exchange uses port 691 (TCP). This port is used for Exchange routing and link state information.

questions.question: Which port does iSCSI use?
questions.explanation: iSCSI (Internet Small Computer System Interface) uses port 860 (TCP). It enables SCSI commands to be sent over IP networks for storage.

questions.question: Which port does VMware Server use?
questions.explanation: VMware Server uses port 902 (TCP/UDP) for remote access to virtual machines and management console communication.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft RPC use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft RPC (Remote Procedure Call) uses port 1025 (TCP). It is used for various Windows services and inter-process communication.

questions.question: Which port does Nessus vulnerability scanner use?
questions.explanation: Nessus vulnerability scanner uses port 1241 (TCP/UDP) for scanner-to-client communication. Nessus is a widely used network vulnerability assessment tool.

questions.question: Which port does Dell OpenManage use?
questions.explanation: Dell OpenManage uses port 1311 (TCP) for its web-based server management interface.

questions.question: Which port does L2TP (Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: L2TP (Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol) uses port 1701 (UDP). It is often combined with IPSec for VPN connections.
questions.takeaway: L2TP = Port 1701 (UDP) — often paired with IPSec for VPN

questions.question: Which port does PPTP (Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: PPTP (Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol) uses port 1723 (TCP/UDP). It is an older VPN protocol developed by Microsoft.

questions.question: Which port does Steam gaming platform use?
questions.explanation: Steam uses port 1725 (UDP) for game client communication. Steam is Valve's digital game distribution platform.

questions.question: Which port does MMS (Microsoft Media Server) use?
questions.explanation: MMS (Microsoft Media Server) uses port 1755 (TCP/UDP). It is used for streaming media with Windows Media Player.

questions.question: Which port does HSRP (Hot Standby Router Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: HSRP (Hot Standby Router Protocol) uses port 1985 (UDP). Cisco's HSRP provides gateway redundancy by allowing multiple routers to share a virtual IP.

questions.question: Which port does Cisco SCCP (Skinny Client Control Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: Cisco SCCP (Skinny Client Control Protocol) uses port 2000 (TCP). It is used for communication between Cisco IP phones and Cisco Unified Communications Manager.
questions.takeaway: Cisco SCCP = Port 2000 (TCP) — Cisco VoIP phones

questions.question: Which port does Cisco ACS (Access Control Server) use?
questions.explanation: Cisco ACS (Access Control Server) uses port 2002 (TCP). It provides centralized authentication and policy control for network access.

questions.question: Which port does NFS (Network File System) use?
questions.explanation: NFS (Network File System) uses port 2049 (TCP/UDP). It allows file sharing across networks, commonly used on Unix/Linux systems.

questions.question: Which port does cPanel web hosting control panel use?
questions.explanation: cPanel uses port 2082 (TCP/UDP) for unsecured access and port 2083 for SSL-secured access to the hosting control panel.

questions.question: Which port does DirectAdmin control panel use?
questions.explanation: DirectAdmin web hosting control panel uses port 2222 (TCP). It is an alternative to cPanel for web hosting management.

questions.question: Which port does CVS (Concurrent Versions System) server use?
questions.explanation: CVS (Concurrent Versions System) server uses port 2401 (TCP). CVS is a version control system used for source code management.

questions.question: Which port does Symantec Antivirus use?
questions.explanation: Symantec Antivirus uses port 2967 (TCP/UDP) for client-server communication in managed antivirus deployments.

questions.question: Which port does iSCSI Target use?
questions.explanation: iSCSI Target uses port 3260 (TCP/UDP). This is the port that iSCSI initiators connect to on the storage target.

questions.question: Which port does SVN (Apache Subversion) use?
questions.explanation: SVN (Apache Subversion) uses port 3690 (TCP/UDP). It is a centralized version control system widely used for source code management.

questions.question: Which port does UPnP (Universal Plug and Play) use?
questions.explanation: UPnP (Universal Plug and Play) uses port 5000 (TCP). It enables devices to discover each other automatically on a network.

questions.question: Which port does iPerf network testing tool use?
questions.explanation: iPerf uses port 5001 (TCP). It is a network performance measurement tool used to test bandwidth between hosts.

questions.question: Which port does mDNS (Multicast DNS) use?
questions.explanation: mDNS (Multicast DNS) uses port 5353 (UDP). It resolves hostnames to IP addresses on small networks without a DNS server (used by Bonjour/Avahi).
questions.takeaway: mDNS = Port 5353 (UDP) — local DNS without server (Bonjour)

questions.question: Which port does CVSup (CVS update) use?
questions.explanation: CVSup uses port 5999 (TCP). It is a network application for distributing and updating collections of files across a network.

questions.question: Which port does Dameware remote control use?
questions.explanation: DameWare remote control software uses port 6129 (TCP). It allows remote access and management of Windows systems.

questions.question: Which port does WebDAV use?
questions.explanation: WebDAV (Web Distributed Authoring and Versioning) uses port 9800 (TCP/UDP). It extends HTTP to allow collaborative editing and file management.

questions.question: Which port does TeamSpeak VoIP use?
questions.explanation: TeamSpeak VoIP server uses port 8767 (UDP). TeamSpeak is a voice communication application commonly used by gamers.

questions.question: Which port does Apache Cassandra database use?
questions.explanation: Apache Cassandra uses port 9042 (TCP) for native protocol client connections. Cassandra is a distributed NoSQL database.

questions.question: Which port does network printing (PDL) use?
questions.explanation: PDL (Page Description Language) network printing uses port 9100 (TCP). This is the standard raw printing port used by network printers.

questions.question: Which port does UserMin web administration use?
questions.explanation: UserMin web-based administration tool uses port 20000 (TCP). It is a web interface for end-user tasks on Unix systems.

questions.question: Which port does Minecraft game server use?
questions.explanation: Minecraft game server uses port 25565 (TCP). This is the default port for Minecraft Java Edition multiplayer servers.

questions.question: Which port does BGMP (Border Gateway Multicast Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: BGMP (Border Gateway Multicast Protocol) uses port 264 (TCP/UDP). It extends BGP to support inter-domain multicast routing.

questions.question: Which port does TSP (Time Stamp Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: TSP (Time Stamp Protocol) uses port 318 (TCP/UDP). It provides trusted timestamping services for documents and transactions.

questions.question: Which port does HP OpenView network management use?
questions.explanation: HP OpenView uses port 381 (TCP/UDP) and port 383 (TCP/UDP) for its network management platform services.

questions.question: Which port does SLP (Service Location Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: SLP (Service Location Protocol) uses port 427 (TCP). It enables computers and other devices to find services on a local area network.

questions.question: Which port does XDMCP (X Display Manager Control Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: XDMCP (X Display Manager Control Protocol) uses port 177 (UDP). It allows remote X Window System display sessions.
questions.takeaway: XDMCP = Port 177 (UDP) — remote X Window sessions

questions.question: Which port does NNTP (Network News Transfer Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: NNTP (Network News Transfer Protocol / Usenet) uses port 119 (TCP). It is the protocol for reading and posting Usenet newsgroup articles.

questions.question: Which port does Ident protocol use?
questions.explanation: Ident protocol uses port 113 (TCP). It identifies the user of a TCP connection and was historically used by IRC servers.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft Exchange ISO-TSAP use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Exchange ISO-TSAP uses port 102 (TCP). This is the ISO Transport Service Access Point used in X.400 mail systems.

questions.question: Which port does Hostname service use?
questions.explanation: Hostname service uses port 101 (TCP). It is a simple service that returns the hostname of the server.

questions.question: Which port does Kerberos (password change) use?
questions.explanation: Kerberos password change uses port 464 (TCP/UDP). This is distinct from port 88 used for Kerberos authentication — port 464 specifically handles the kpasswd service.

questions.question: Which port does Dantz Retrospect backup use?
questions.explanation: Dantz Retrospect backup software uses port 497 (TCP/UDP) for client-server backup communication.

questions.question: Which port does UUCP (Unix-to-Unix Copy Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: UUCP (Unix-to-Unix Copy Protocol) uses port 540 (TCP). It is one of the oldest Unix networking protocols for transferring files.

questions.question: Which port does DHCPv6 client use?
questions.explanation: DHCPv6 client uses port 546 (TCP/UDP). DHCPv6 provides dynamic IPv6 address assignment, with the server listening on port 547.
questions.takeaway: DHCPv6 Client = Port 546 | DHCPv6 Server = Port 547 (TCP/UDP)

questions.question: Which port does rmonitor use?
questions.explanation: rmonitor uses port 560 (UDP). It is used for remote network monitoring purposes.

questions.question: Which port does FileMaker database use?
questions.explanation: FileMaker Pro database server uses port 591 (TCP). FileMaker is a cross-platform relational database application from Claris.

questions.question: Which port does SMSD (SMS Daemon) use?
questions.explanation: SMSD (SMS Daemon) uses port 596 (TCP/UDP). It handles SMS gateway communication in some network implementations.

questions.question: Which port does IPP (Internet Printing Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: IPP (Internet Printing Protocol) uses port 631 (TCP). It is the modern standard for network printing used by CUPS on Linux/macOS.
questions.takeaway: IPP = Port 631 (TCP) — CUPS / modern network printing

questions.question: Which port does MSDP (PIM) use?
questions.explanation: MSDP (Multicast Source Discovery Protocol / PIM) uses port 639 (TCP). It is used with PIM-SM for inter-domain multicast routing.

questions.question: Which port does LDP (MPLS Label Distribution Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: LDP (Label Distribution Protocol) for MPLS uses port 646 (TCP/UDP). It distributes label-to-FEC bindings in MPLS networks.

questions.question: Which port does H.323 gatestat use?
questions.explanation: H.323 Gatestat uses port 1719 (UDP). H.323 is a VoIP signaling standard, and port 1719 handles gatekeeper discovery.

questions.question: Which port does H.323 Hostcall use?
questions.explanation: H.323 Hostcall uses port 1720 (TCP). This is the main H.323 call signaling port for VoIP systems.

questions.question: Which port does RADIUS Accounting use?
questions.explanation: RADIUS Accounting uses port 1813 (TCP/UDP). It tracks network usage data for billing and reporting purposes.

questions.question: Which port does TeamSpeak 3 Accounting use?
questions.explanation: TeamSpeak 3 Accounting server uses port 2008 (TCP) for license and accounting communication.

questions.question: Which port does TeamSpeak 3 Web List use?
questions.explanation: TeamSpeak 3 Web List uses port 2010 (UDP). It allows the TeamSpeak client to retrieve public server lists.

questions.question: Which port does rwhois (Referral WHOIS) use?
questions.explanation: rwhois (Referral WHOIS) uses port 4321 (TCP). It is a distributed WHOIS system for distributed registration data.

questions.question: Which port does mSQL database use?
questions.explanation: mSQL (Mini SQL) database uses port 4333 (TCP). It is a lightweight relational database management system.

questions.question: Which port does Radmin remote administration use?
questions.explanation: Radmin (Remote Administrator) uses port 4899 (TCP). It provides remote desktop and file transfer capabilities for Windows systems.

questions.question: Which port does RTP/RTSP media use?
questions.explanation: RTP/RTSP streaming media uses ports 5004-5005 (UDP). RTP (Real-time Transport Protocol) carries audio/video data for streaming.

questions.question: Which port does SIP-TLS (secure VoIP signaling) use?
questions.explanation: SIP-TLS uses port 5061 (TCP). It is the encrypted version of SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) for secure VoIP signaling.

questions.question: Which port does XMPP (Jabber) use?
questions.explanation: XMPP (Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol / Jabber) uses port 5222 (TCP) for client connections and ports 5222-5223 for secure connections.

questions.question: Which port does VNC over HTTP use?
questions.explanation: VNC over HTTP uses port 5800 (TCP). This allows VNC remote desktop access through a web browser without a dedicated VNC client.
questions.takeaway: VNC over HTTP = Port 5800 (TCP) — browser-based VNC

questions.question: Which port does X11 windowing system use?
questions.explanation: X11 (X Window System) uses ports 6000-6001 (TCP). It provides the graphical display framework for Unix/Linux systems.

questions.question: Which port does DAAP (iTunes Music Sharing) use?
questions.explanation: DAAP (Digital Audio Access Protocol) uses port 3689 (TCP). It is Apple's protocol for sharing music libraries in iTunes/Music app.

questions.question: Which port does Ingreslock use?
questions.explanation: Ingreslock uses port 1524 (TCP/UDP). It was historically exploited as a backdoor vulnerability in Ingres database systems.
questions.takeaway: Ingreslock = Port 1524 (TCP/UDP) — historically used as backdoor

questions.question: Which port does Cisco VQP (VLAN Query Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: Cisco VQP (VLAN Query Protocol) uses port 1589 (TCP/UDP). It is used with VMPS (VLAN Membership Policy Server) for dynamic VLAN assignment.
questions.takeaway: Cisco VQP = Port 1589 (TCP/UDP) — dynamic VLAN assignment

questions.question: Which port does WINS (Windows Internet Name Service) use?
questions.explanation: WINS (Windows Internet Name Service) uses port 1512 (TCP/UDP). It resolves NetBIOS names to IP addresses in Windows environments.

questions.question: Which port does Citrix ICA (Independent Computing Architecture) use?
questions.explanation: Citrix ICA (Independent Computing Architecture) uses port 1494 (TCP). It is the protocol used by Citrix for virtual application and desktop delivery.

questions.question: Which port does Oracle database use?
questions.explanation: Oracle database uses port 2483 (TCP/UDP) for unencrypted connections and port 2484 (TCP/UDP) for SSL-encrypted connections.

questions.question: Which port does Skype use?
questions.explanation: Skype uses port 23399 (TCP/UDP). Skype is Microsoft's VoIP and messaging application.

questions.question: Which port does traceroute use?
questions.explanation: Traceroute uses port 33434 (TCP/UDP) as its starting port. It sends probes with incrementing TTL values to discover the network path.

questions.question: Which port does wnn6 (Japanese input) use?
questions.explanation: wnn6 (Japanese input method server) uses port 22273 (TCP/UDP). It provides Japanese character input services.

questions.question: Which port does BPRD (Backup Pro Remote Data) use?
questions.explanation: BPRD uses port 13720 (TCP/UDP). It is used by Veritas NetBackup for remote data operations.

questions.question: Which port does BPDBm (Backup Database Manager) use?
questions.explanation: BPDBm (Backup Product Database Manager) uses port 13721 (TCP/UDP). It is part of the Veritas NetBackup suite.

questions.question: Which port does vnetd (Veritas Network Daemon) use?
questions.explanation: vnetd (Veritas Network Daemon) uses port 13724 (TCP/UDP). It handles network communication for Veritas backup products.

questions.question: Which port does BPCd (Backup Process Control Daemon) use?
questions.explanation: BPCd uses port 13782 (TCP/UDP). It is the backup process control daemon for Veritas NetBackup.

questions.question: Which port does vopied (Veritas OPTIONSd) use?
questions.explanation: vopied uses port 13783 (TCP/UDP). It is part of the Veritas backup software suite for license management.

questions.question: Which port does corbaloc (CORBA) use?
questions.explanation: corbaloc (CORBA Location Service) uses port 2809 (TCP/UDP). CORBA is a standard for distributed object-oriented computing.

questions.question: Which port does zephyr-clt use?
questions.explanation: Zephyr-clt uses port 2103 (TCP/UDP). Zephyr is a real-time messaging and notification system developed at MIT.

questions.question: Which port does zephyr-hm use?
questions.explanation: Zephyr-hm (Host Manager) uses port 2104 (TCP/UDP). It manages host subscriptions in the Zephyr notification system.

questions.question: Which port does zephyr-srv use?
questions.explanation: Zephyr-srv (Zephyr Server) uses port 2101 (TCP/UDP). It is the main Zephyr notification server.

questions.question: Which port does SMTP Submission (with STARTTLS) use?
questions.explanation: SMTP Submission with STARTTLS uses port 587 (TCP). It is the recommended port for email clients to submit outgoing email to their mail server.

questions.question: Which port does VNC (Virtual Network Computing) primary display use?
questions.explanation: VNC primary display uses port 5900 (TCP). VNC allows remote graphical desktop control across platforms.

questions.question: Which port does the rlogin service use?
questions.explanation: rlogin (remote login) uses port 513 (TCP). It allows users to log into a remote Unix host — replaced by SSH for security.

questions.question: Which port does GLBP (Gateway Load Balancing Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: GLBP (Cisco Gateway Load Balancing Protocol) uses port 3222 (TCP/UDP). It provides redundancy and load balancing across multiple gateway routers.
questions.takeaway: GLBP = Port 3222 (TCP/UDP) — Cisco gateway load balancing

questions.question: Which port does Analogx proxy use?
questions.explanation: Analogx proxy uses port 6588 (TCP). It is a lightweight proxy server software for Windows.

questions.question: Which port number range is assigned to ephemeral (dynamic) ports?
questions.explanation: Ephemeral (dynamic/private) ports use the range 49152-65535. These are temporary ports assigned by the OS for client-side connections.
questions.takeaway: Ephemeral ports = 49152-65535 (dynamic, assigned by OS for clients)

questions.question: Which port number range is assigned to registered ports?
questions.explanation: Registered ports use the range 1024-49151. These are registered with IANA for specific applications but do not require root/admin privileges.

questions.question: Which port does IMAPS (IMAP Secure) use?
questions.explanation: IMAPS (IMAP over SSL/TLS) uses port 993 (TCP). It provides encrypted access to email on the mail server.

questions.question: Which port does POP3S (POP3 Secure) use?
questions.explanation: POP3S (POP3 over SSL/TLS) uses port 995 (TCP/UDP). It provides encrypted email download from a mail server.

questions.question: Which port does DAAP (Digital Audio Access Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: DAAP uses port 3689 (TCP). Apple's iTunes/Music app uses DAAP to share music libraries over local networks.

questions.question: Which port does VMware Server management use?
questions.explanation: VMware Server uses port 8200 (TCP/UDP) for its management interface. VMware also uses port 8222 for additional services.

questions.question: Which protocol and port does traceroute on Windows (using ICMP) differ from Linux (using UDP)?
options: Windows uses TCP; Linux uses UDP
Windows uses ICMP; Linux uses UDP starting at 33434
Windows uses UDP 33434; Linux uses ICMP
questions.explanation: Windows tracert uses ICMP Echo Requests. Linux traceroute uses UDP packets starting at port 33434 by default. Both increment TTL to map hops.

questions.question: Which port does SNMP agent (unencrypted) listen on?
questions.explanation: SNMP agents (unencrypted) listen on port 161 (UDP). Port 10161 is used for SNMP over TLS (encrypted).

questions.question: Which port does SNMP trap (unencrypted) use?
questions.explanation: SNMP traps (unencrypted) are sent to port 162 (UDP). Port 10162 is used for SNMP traps over TLS.

questions.question: Which port does SNMP agent over TLS use?
questions.explanation: SNMP agent over TLS (encrypted SNMP) uses port 10161 (TCP). This provides secure SNMP management communication.

questions.question: Which port does SNMP trap over TLS use?
questions.explanation: SNMP trap over TLS uses port 10162 (TCP). It provides encrypted delivery of SNMP alert messages.

questions.question: Which port does SSH use and what protocol?
questions.explanation: SSH uses TCP port 22. TCP is used because SSH requires reliable, ordered delivery for interactive sessions and file transfers.

questions.question: Which port does DNS use for standard queries?
options: UDP 53 for queries, TCP 53 for zone transfers
questions.explanation: DNS uses UDP port 53 for standard queries (faster, no connection overhead) and TCP port 53 for zone transfers and responses larger than 512 bytes.
questions.takeaway: DNS queries = UDP 53 | DNS zone transfers = TCP 53

questions.question: Which port does MS SQL Server Browser service use?
questions.explanation: MS SQL Server Browser uses port 1434 (TCP/UDP). It listens for incoming requests and provides information about SQL Server instances and their ports.

questions.question: Which port does Oracle database SSL use?
questions.explanation: Oracle database SSL connections use port 2484 (TCP/UDP). Standard unencrypted Oracle connections use port 2483.

questions.question: FTP uses two ports — which combination is correct?
questions.explanation: FTP uses port 20 for data transfer and port 21 for control commands. The control connection on port 21 manages commands; the data connection on port 20 transfers files.
questions.takeaway: FTP Data = Port 20 | FTP Control = Port 21

questions.question: Which port does HTTP Proxy (alternative) use besides 8080?
questions.explanation: Port 3128 (TCP/UDP) is another common HTTP proxy port, notably used by Squid proxy server. Port 8080 is also commonly used.

questions.question: Which port does LDAP over TLS/SSL (LDAPS) use?
questions.explanation: LDAPS (LDAP over SSL/TLS) uses port 636 (TCP/UDP). It provides encrypted LDAP directory service queries, as opposed to unencrypted LDAP on port 389.

questions.question: Which port does Global Catalog LDAP use in Active Directory?
questions.explanation: Active Directory Global Catalog LDAP uses port 3268 (TCP). The SSL version uses port 3269. Global Catalog allows searching across all domains in a forest.
questions.takeaway: AD Global Catalog LDAP = Port 3268 | SSL = Port 3269

questions.question: Which port does RADIUS authentication use?
questions.explanation: RADIUS authentication uses port 1812 (TCP/UDP). The older RADIUS port was 1645. Port 1813 is used for RADIUS accounting.

questions.question: Which well-known port does HTTPS use?
questions.explanation: HTTPS uses the well-known port 443 (TCP/UDP/SCTP). Any deviation from this (like 8443) is a non-standard configuration.

questions.question: Which port does Telnet use?
questions.explanation: Telnet uses port 23 (TCP). It is an unencrypted text-based remote login protocol. Always use SSH (port 22) instead for security.
questions.takeaway: Telnet = Port 23 (TCP) — unencrypted, use SSH instead

questions.question: Which port does OpenVPN use by default?
questions.explanation: OpenVPN defaults to port 1194 (TCP/UDP). It can also be configured to use port 443 (TCP) to bypass firewalls.
questions.takeaway: OpenVPN = Port 1194 (TCP/UDP) [can use 443 to bypass firewalls]

questions.question: Which port does L2TP/IPSec VPN use?
questions.explanation: L2TP uses port 1701 (UDP) for tunneling. IPSec uses port 500 (UDP) for IKE key exchange and port 4500 (UDP) for NAT traversal.

questions.question: Which port does XDMCP use for remote X sessions?
questions.explanation: XDMCP (X Display Manager Control Protocol) uses port 177 (UDP). It enables remote graphical login sessions for X Window System.
questions.takeaway: XDMCP = Port 177 (UDP) — remote X Window login

questions.question: Which port does AFP (Apple Filing Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: AFP uses port 548 (TCP). It is Apple's native file sharing protocol used on macOS for sharing files between Macs.

questions.question: Which port does SOCKS proxy use?
questions.explanation: SOCKS proxy uses port 1080 (TCP/UDP). SOCKS5 supports TCP and UDP protocols and authentication, commonly used for bypassing restrictions.

questions.question: Which port does MySQL use?
questions.explanation: MySQL relational database uses port 3306 (TCP/UDP). This is the default port for MySQL server connections.

questions.question: Which port does PostgreSQL use?
questions.explanation: PostgreSQL relational database uses port 5432 (TCP). This is the default port for PostgreSQL server connections.

questions.question: Which port does MongoDB use?
questions.explanation: MongoDB NoSQL database uses port 27017 (TCP/UDP). The web status interface uses port 28017.

questions.question: Which port does Redis use?
questions.explanation: Redis in-memory data store uses port 6379 (TCP). Redis is used as a cache, session store, and message broker.

questions.question: Which port does Cassandra use for native protocol?
questions.explanation: Apache Cassandra uses port 9042 (TCP) for native protocol CQL queries. Port 7000 is used for inter-node communication.

questions.question: Which port does Elasticsearch use for REST API?
questions.explanation: Elasticsearch uses port 9200 (TCP) for its REST API (HTTP). Port 9300 is used for inter-node transport communication.
questions.takeaway: Elasticsearch REST API = Port 9200 | Transport = Port 9300

questions.question: Which port does RabbitMQ AMQP use?
questions.explanation: RabbitMQ uses port 5672 (TCP) for AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol) connections. Port 5671 is used for AMQP over TLS. Port 15672 is the management UI.
questions.takeaway: RabbitMQ AMQP = Port 5672 | TLS = Port 5671 | UI = Port 15672

questions.question: Which port does Apache ZooKeeper use for client connections?
questions.explanation: Apache ZooKeeper uses port 2181 (TCP) for client connections. ZooKeeper is a distributed coordination service for distributed applications.

questions.question: Which port does Kafka broker use by default?
questions.explanation: Apache Kafka broker uses port 9092 (TCP) by default for producer and consumer connections. Port 9093 is used for SSL connections.

questions.question: Which port does Grafana monitoring dashboard use?
questions.explanation: Grafana uses port 3000 (TCP) for its web dashboard. Grafana is an open-source analytics and monitoring platform.

questions.question: Which port does Prometheus monitoring use?
questions.explanation: Prometheus monitoring system uses port 9090 (TCP) for its web UI and API. It scrapes metrics from configured targets.

questions.question: Which port does Jenkins CI/CD use by default?
questions.explanation: Jenkins CI/CD server uses port 8080 (TCP) by default. Jenkins is an open-source automation server for building, testing, and deploying software.

questions.question: Which port does Docker daemon use for remote API?
questions.explanation: Docker daemon uses port 2375 (TCP) for unencrypted remote API access and port 2376 for TLS-encrypted access. Port 2377 is used for Docker Swarm cluster management.

questions.question: Which port does Kubernetes API server use?
questions.explanation: Kubernetes API server uses port 6443 (TCP). This is the main port used by kubectl and other clients to communicate with the cluster.

questions.question: Which port does etcd (Kubernetes key-value store) use for client communication?
questions.explanation: etcd uses port 2379 (TCP) for client requests and port 2380 (TCP) for peer-to-peer communication. etcd stores Kubernetes cluster state.

questions.question: Which port does WireGuard VPN use by default?
questions.explanation: WireGuard VPN uses port 51820 (UDP) by default. WireGuard is a modern, fast, and secure VPN protocol built into the Linux kernel.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft Active Directory Kerberos use for password change?
questions.explanation: Active Directory Kerberos password change uses port 464 (TCP/UDP). This is the kpasswd service port, distinct from the main Kerberos authentication port 88.

questions.question: Which port does MSRPC (Microsoft Remote Procedure Call) dynamically assign beyond port 135?
questions.explanation: After the initial EPMAP negotiation on port 135, Microsoft RPC dynamically assigns ports from the ephemeral range 49152-65535 (TCP) for actual RPC service communication.

questions.question: Which port does SNMP over TLS (secure SNMP) use for traps?
questions.explanation: SNMP traps over TLS use port 10162 (TCP). This is the secure version of SNMP trap port 162, providing encrypted alert delivery.

questions.question: Which port does BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) use and what transport protocol?
questions.explanation: BGP uses TCP port 179. TCP is required because BGP needs reliable, ordered delivery for routing table updates between autonomous systems.
questions.takeaway: BGP = TCP Port 179 (TCP required for reliable routing updates)

questions.question: Which port does IPSec NAT Traversal (NAT-T) use?
questions.explanation: IPSec NAT Traversal uses port 4500 (UDP). When IPSec traffic passes through a NAT device, it is encapsulated in UDP on port 4500 to avoid NAT compatibility issues.

questions.question: Which port does SMTPS (SMTP over SSL) use?
questions.explanation: SMTPS (SMTP over SSL/TLS) uses port 465 (TCP). Note: port 587 with STARTTLS is the modern recommended submission port, while 465 is the implicit TLS version.
questions.takeaway: SMTPS = Port 465 (TCP) implicit TLS | Port 587 = STARTTLS submission

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft SQL Server Analysis Services use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft SQL Server Analysis Services (SSAS) uses port 2383 (TCP). It is used for online analytical processing (OLAP) and data mining.

questions.question: Which port does FTPS (implicit FTP over TLS) control channel use?
questions.explanation: FTPS implicit mode uses port 990 (TCP) for control and port 989 (TCP) for data. In implicit mode, TLS is established immediately upon connection.
questions.takeaway: FTPS control = Port 990 (TCP) | FTPS data = Port 989 (TCP)

questions.question: Which port does RTSP (Real Time Streaming Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: RTSP uses port 554 (TCP/UDP). It controls streaming media sessions — like a TV remote for streams — but does not deliver the actual media data.
questions.takeaway: RTSP = Port 554 (TCP/UDP) — controls streams, not data delivery

questions.question: Which port does Webmin system administration use?
questions.explanation: Webmin uses port 10000 (TCP) for its web-based Unix/Linux system administration interface.

questions.question: Which port does RDP (Remote Desktop Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: RDP (Remote Desktop Protocol) uses port 3389 (TCP). It enables graphical remote access to Windows systems.

questions.question: Which port does HTTP alternate (commonly used for web testing) use?
questions.explanation: Port 8080 (TCP) is the most common alternate HTTP port for web proxies, development servers, and testing environments when port 80 is not available.
questions.takeaway: HTTP alternate = Port 8080 (TCP) — proxies and dev servers

questions.question: Which port does iSCSI use for target connections?
questions.explanation: iSCSI target uses port 3260 (TCP/UDP). iSCSI initiators connect to this port on storage targets. Port 860 is the older iSCSI port.

questions.question: Which port does XMPP (Jabber) server-to-server communication use?
questions.explanation: XMPP server-to-server federation uses port 5269 (TCP). Client-to-server connections use port 5222. This separation allows servers to communicate directly.
questions.takeaway: XMPP client = Port 5222 | XMPP server-to-server = Port 5269

questions.question: Which port does the rsync daemon use?
questions.explanation: The rsync daemon uses port 873 (TCP). rsync is a fast file synchronization tool commonly used for backups and mirroring.

questions.question: Which port does NFS (Network File System) use?
questions.explanation: NFS uses port 2049 (TCP/UDP). It is the standard file sharing protocol for Unix/Linux systems allowing remote filesystem access.

questions.question: Which port does Kerberos authentication use?
questions.explanation: Kerberos authentication uses port 88 (TCP/UDP). It is the primary authentication protocol in Active Directory environments.

questions.question: Which port does LDAP use?
questions.explanation: LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) uses port 389 (TCP/UDP). It accesses directory services like Microsoft Active Directory.

questions.question: Which port does BGP use?
questions.explanation: BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) uses port 179 (TCP). It is the routing protocol that powers internet routing between autonomous systems.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft Active Directory Global Catalog use over SSL?
questions.explanation: Active Directory Global Catalog over SSL uses port 3269 (TCP). The non-SSL Global Catalog uses port 3268. Global Catalog searches all objects in an AD forest.

questions.question: Which port does Cisco VQP (VMPS) use?
questions.explanation: Cisco VMPS (VLAN Membership Policy Server) uses VQP on port 1589 (TCP/UDP) to dynamically assign switch ports to VLANs based on MAC addresses.

questions.question: Which port does IGMP (Internet Group Management Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: IGMP does not use a port number — it uses IP protocol number 2. IGMP manages multicast group membership between hosts and routers.
questions.takeaway: IGMP = No port, uses IP protocol number 2 (multicast groups)

questions.question: Which port does GRE (Generic Routing Encapsulation) use?
questions.explanation: GRE does not use a port number — it uses IP protocol number 47. GRE is a tunneling protocol used to encapsulate packets within IP tunnels.
questions.takeaway: GRE = No port, uses IP protocol number 47 (tunneling)

questions.question: Which port does ESP (Encapsulating Security Payload / IPSec) use?
questions.explanation: ESP (Encapsulating Security Payload) uses IP protocol number 50, not a port number. It provides confidentiality, integrity, and authentication for IPSec.
questions.takeaway: ESP (IPSec) = No port, uses IP protocol number 50

questions.question: Which port does AH (Authentication Header / IPSec) use?
questions.explanation: AH (Authentication Header) uses IP protocol number 51, not a port number. It provides integrity and authentication for IPSec without encryption.
questions.takeaway: AH (IPSec) = No port, uses IP protocol number 51

questions.question: Which port does Grafana Loki (log aggregation) use?
questions.explanation: Grafana Loki uses port 3100 (TCP) for its HTTP API. Loki is a horizontally scalable log aggregation system inspired by Prometheus.

questions.question: Which port does HashiCorp Vault use?
questions.explanation: HashiCorp Vault uses port 8200 (TCP) for its API and web UI. Vault is a secrets management tool for storing and accessing credentials.

questions.question: Which port does Consul (service discovery) use for HTTP API?
questions.explanation: HashiCorp Consul uses port 8500 (TCP) for its HTTP API and web UI, and port 8600 (UDP/TCP) for DNS queries.
questions.takeaway: Consul HTTP API = Port 8500 | DNS = Port 8600

questions.question: Which port does TFTP use and why UDP?
questions.explanation: TFTP uses UDP port 69. UDP is used because TFTP is designed for simple, fast transfers (like booting network devices) where the overhead of TCP connection setup is undesirable.
questions.takeaway: TFTP = UDP Port 69 (simple, fast, no TCP overhead — used for network booting)

questions.question: Which port does a web proxy server typically use?
questions.explanation: Web proxy servers typically use port 8080 (TCP). This is one of the most common alternative ports for HTTP proxying when port 80 is unavailable.

questions.question: Which port does SSH file transfer (SFTP) use?
questions.explanation: SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) uses port 22 (TCP) — the same as SSH. SFTP is not a separate protocol from SSH; it runs as a subsystem within an SSH connection.
questions.takeaway: SFTP = Port 22 (TCP) — runs within SSH connection

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft Exchange OWA (Outlook Web Access) use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft Exchange OWA (Outlook Web Access) uses port 443 (HTTPS). Web-based email access is always served over HTTPS for security.

questions.question: Which port does NetFlow (Cisco network traffic monitoring) use?
questions.explanation: NetFlow uses port 2055 (UDP) by default. NetFlow is Cisco's protocol for collecting and monitoring IP network traffic flow data.

questions.question: Which port does sFlow (traffic monitoring) use?
questions.explanation: sFlow uses port 6343 (UDP). sFlow is a multi-vendor traffic monitoring standard for network devices, competing with NetFlow.

questions.question: Which port does IPFIX (IP Flow Information Export) use?
questions.explanation: IPFIX uses port 4739 (TCP/UDP). IPFIX (RFC 5101) is the IETF standard for exporting IP flow information, based on Cisco's NetFlow v9.

questions.question: Which port does OpenLDAP use?
questions.explanation: OpenLDAP uses the standard LDAP port 389 (TCP/UDP) for unencrypted directory access, and port 636 for LDAPS (LDAP over SSL).

questions.question: Which port does SMTP use for sending email?
questions.explanation: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) uses port 25 (TCP) as its standard port. Port 587 is used for email submission by clients with authentication.
questions.takeaway: SMTP = Port 25 (TCP) server-to-server | Port 587 = client submission

questions.question: Which port does NTP (Network Time Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: NTP uses port 123 (UDP). It is critical for synchronizing time across all network devices, especially for logging, certificates, and authentication.

questions.question: Which port does RADIUS use for old/legacy authentication?
questions.explanation: The legacy RADIUS authentication port is 1645 (TCP/UDP). This was the original port before IANA standardized it to port 1812.
questions.takeaway: RADIUS legacy = Port 1645 | RADIUS current = Port 1812

questions.question: Which port does TACACS+ use?
questions.explanation: TACACS+ (Terminal Access Controller Access Control System Plus) uses port 49 (TCP). Unlike the original TACACS which uses UDP, TACACS+ uses TCP for reliable communication.
questions.takeaway: TACACS+ = Port 49 (TCP) — Cisco's enhanced AAA protocol

questions.question: Which port does Syslog over TLS (secure syslog) use?
questions.explanation: Syslog over TLS uses port 6514 (TCP). It provides encrypted transport for syslog messages, as standard syslog on port 514 uses unencrypted UDP.
questions.takeaway: Syslog TLS = Port 6514 (TCP) | Syslog standard = Port 514 (UDP)

questions.question: Which port does SLP (Service Location Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: SLP (Service Location Protocol) uses port 427 (TCP/UDP). It allows computers to find services like printers and file servers automatically on a local network.

questions.question: Which port does mDNS (used by Apple Bonjour) use?
questions.explanation: mDNS (Multicast DNS) used by Apple Bonjour uses port 5353 (UDP). It enables zero-configuration service discovery on local networks without a DNS server.

questions.question: Which port does Splunk's default web interface use?
questions.explanation: Splunk's default web interface uses port 8000 (TCP). Splunk is a popular SIEM and log analytics platform.

questions.question: Which port does Elasticsearch cluster transport use?
questions.explanation: Elasticsearch uses port 9300 (TCP) for inter-node transport (cluster communication). Port 9200 is used for the HTTP REST API.
questions.takeaway: Elasticsearch transport = Port 9300 (TCP) | REST API = Port 9200

questions.question: Which port does OCSP (Online Certificate Status Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: OCSP (Online Certificate Status Protocol) uses port 80 (HTTP) or 443 (HTTPS). OCSP responders are typically accessed via HTTP/HTTPS URLs embedded in certificates.

questions.question: Which port does MPLS LDP (Label Distribution Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: MPLS LDP (Label Distribution Protocol) uses port 646 (TCP/UDP). LDP is used to distribute MPLS labels between routers in an MPLS network.

questions.question: In CDAC CCEE context, which port does HTTP use?
questions.explanation: HTTP uses port 80. This is one of the most commonly tested port numbers in CDAC CCEE networking exams.
questions.takeaway: HTTP = Port 80 (TCP) — most tested port in CCEE

questions.question: Which port does HTTPS use for secure web communication?
questions.explanation: HTTPS uses port 443. It encrypts HTTP using TLS/SSL. Frequently tested in CDAC CCEE FCN module.

questions.question: Which port is used by SSH for secure remote login?
questions.explanation: SSH uses port 22 (TCP). It replaces Telnet and provides encrypted remote access. Very commonly tested in CCEE.

questions.question: Which port does FTP use for the control connection?
questions.explanation: FTP control connection uses port 21 (TCP). The data connection uses port 20. Both ports are commonly tested in CCEE.

questions.question: Which port does Telnet use for remote login?
questions.explanation: Telnet uses port 23 (TCP). It is unencrypted and insecure — replaced by SSH. Commonly tested in CCEE to compare with SSH.

questions.question: Which port does SMTP use to send emails?
questions.explanation: SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) uses port 25 (TCP) for server-to-server email. Port 587 is for client submission.

questions.question: Which port does DNS use to resolve domain names?
questions.explanation: DNS uses port 53 (TCP/UDP). UDP for standard queries, TCP for zone transfers. Critical for all CCEE networking topics.
questions.takeaway: DNS = Port 53 (UDP for queries, TCP for zone transfers)

questions.question: Which port does DHCP server use to assign IP addresses?
questions.explanation: DHCP server uses port 67 (UDP). The DHCP client uses port 68. DHCP automates IP address assignment — commonly tested in CCEE.

questions.question: Which port does TFTP use?
questions.explanation: TFTP (Trivial FTP) uses port 69 (UDP). It is simpler than FTP with no authentication, used for booting network devices.
questions.takeaway: TFTP = Port 69 (UDP) — simple, no auth, network booting

questions.question: Which port does POP3 use to receive emails?
questions.explanation: POP3 (Post Office Protocol v3) uses port 110 (TCP). It downloads email from server to client. CCEE frequently asks to compare POP3 vs IMAP.

questions.question: Which port does IMAP use to access emails on the server?
questions.explanation: IMAP (Internet Message Access Protocol) uses port 143 (TCP). Unlike POP3, IMAP keeps mail on the server — common CCEE comparison question.
questions.takeaway: IMAP = Port 143 (email on server, sync across devices)

questions.question: Which port does NTP use for time synchronization?
questions.explanation: NTP (Network Time Protocol) uses port 123 (UDP). Critical for certificates, logs, and authentication — commonly tested in CCEE security topics.

questions.question: Which port does SNMP use for network management?
questions.explanation: SNMP (Simple Network Management Protocol) uses port 161 (UDP) for agent communication. Port 162 is used for SNMP traps.

questions.question: Which port does RDP use for Windows remote desktop?
questions.explanation: RDP (Remote Desktop Protocol) uses port 3389 (TCP). It is Windows' built-in remote desktop protocol — frequently tested in CCEE security module.

questions.question: Which port does MySQL database use?
questions.explanation: MySQL uses port 3306 (TCP). This is one of the most commonly tested database ports in CCEE exams alongside PostgreSQL (5432) and MSSQL (1433).

questions.question: Which protocol does DNS primarily use for regular queries and why?
options: UDP — for speed and low overhead
questions.explanation: DNS uses UDP (port 53) for regular queries because UDP is faster with less overhead. TCP is used for zone transfers and responses >512 bytes.
questions.takeaway: DNS queries = UDP 53 (fast) | Zone transfers = TCP 53

questions.question: Which protocol does DHCP use and why?
options: UDP — because clients have no IP yet
questions.explanation: DHCP uses UDP (ports 67/68) because clients don't have an IP address yet and can't establish a TCP connection — they must broadcast.
questions.takeaway: DHCP = UDP (clients have no IP yet, must broadcast)

questions.question: Which protocol does TFTP use and why?
questions.explanation: TFTP uses UDP (port 69) because it's designed for simple, fast transfers where TCP connection setup overhead is undesirable.
questions.takeaway: TFTP = UDP 69 (simple, fast, no TCP handshake)

questions.question: Which of the following uses both TCP AND UDP on the same port?
questions.explanation: DNS uses both TCP and UDP on port 53 — UDP for standard queries, TCP for zone transfers and large responses. This dual-protocol use is commonly tested in CCEE.
questions.takeaway: DNS = port 53 on both TCP (zone transfers) and UDP (queries)

questions.question: SSH uses which transport protocol and port?
questions.explanation: SSH uses TCP port 22. TCP is required for SSH because it needs reliable, ordered, error-checked delivery for interactive terminal sessions.

questions.question: Which port does BGP use and on which transport protocol?
questions.explanation: BGP (Border Gateway Protocol) uses TCP port 179. TCP is required for BGP because routing table updates must be reliably delivered between autonomous systems.

questions.question: Which port does RIP (Routing Information Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: RIP uses UDP port 520. It broadcasts routing updates every 30 seconds. UDP is used because RIP doesn't need guaranteed delivery for routing updates.

questions.question: Which port does OSPF use?
questions.explanation: OSPF doesn't use a port number — it uses IP protocol number 89. It communicates directly over IP, not TCP or UDP. Commonly confused with BGP (port 179).
questions.takeaway: OSPF = IP protocol number 89 (no TCP/UDP port)

questions.question: Which port does EIGRP use?
questions.explanation: EIGRP uses IP protocol number 88, not a TCP/UDP port. It communicates directly over IP. Commonly tested in CCEE to distinguish from other routing protocols.
questions.takeaway: EIGRP = IP protocol number 88 (no TCP/UDP port)

questions.question: Which port does ICMP use?
questions.explanation: ICMP uses IP protocol number 1 — it has no port number. ICMP carries control messages like ping (echo request/reply). Frequently tested in CCEE.
questions.takeaway: ICMP = IP protocol 1 (no port) — used by ping, traceroute

questions.question: At which OSI layer do port numbers operate?
questions.explanation: Port numbers are a Layer 4 (Transport Layer) concept. TCP and UDP use port numbers to identify specific applications/services on a host.

questions.question: HTTP operates at which OSI layer?
questions.explanation: HTTP operates at Layer 7 (Application Layer) of the OSI model. Port 80 is its well-known port at Layer 4.

questions.question: Which OSI layer is responsible for establishing, maintaining, and terminating sessions?
questions.explanation: The Session Layer (Layer 5) manages sessions between applications. NetBIOS and PPTP operate at this layer.
questions.takeaway: Session Layer = Layer 5 (establish, maintain, terminate sessions)

questions.question: TLS/SSL which secures HTTPS operates at which OSI layer?
questions.explanation: TLS/SSL operates between Layer 4 and Layer 7 — typically categorized at Layer 6 (Presentation) as it handles encryption and data format translation.

questions.question: What is the port number range for well-known (system) ports?
questions.explanation: Well-known ports range from 0 to 1023. These are assigned by IANA for standard services like HTTP (80), HTTPS (443), SSH (22), FTP (21).

questions.question: What is the maximum port number possible in TCP/UDP?
questions.explanation: Port numbers are 16-bit unsigned integers, giving a range of 0 to 65535. Port 65535 is the maximum possible port number.

questions.question: Which port range is used for registered ports (not system, not ephemeral)?
questions.explanation: Registered ports (1024-49151) are registered with IANA for specific applications but don't require root/system privileges to use.

questions.question: Which port range is used for ephemeral (client-side) ports?
questions.explanation: Ephemeral ports (49152-65535) are dynamically assigned by the OS for client-side connections. They are temporary and released after the connection closes.

questions.question: Which port does FTP Data use?
questions.explanation: FTP data transfer uses port 20 (TCP/SCTP). The FTP control channel uses port 21. Both are needed for file transfer.
questions.takeaway: FTP Data = Port 20 | FTP Control = Port 21

questions.question: Which port does LDAP use for directory services?
questions.explanation: LDAP (Lightweight Directory Access Protocol) uses port 389 (TCP/UDP). It's used to access Active Directory and other directory services.

questions.question: Which port does LDAPS (LDAP over SSL) use?
questions.explanation: LDAPS uses port 636 (TCP/UDP). It is the encrypted version of LDAP using SSL/TLS, important for secure Active Directory communication.

questions.question: Which port does Kerberos use for authentication?
questions.explanation: Kerberos uses port 88 (TCP/UDP). It is the authentication protocol used in Microsoft Active Directory and many enterprise environments.

questions.question: Which port does SMB (Windows file sharing) use?
questions.explanation: SMB (Server Message Block) uses port 445 (TCP). Port 139 was the older NetBIOS-based SMB. Port 445 is direct TCP SMB used in modern Windows.
questions.takeaway: SMB modern = Port 445 | NetBIOS SMB legacy = Port 139

questions.question: Which port does SMTP Submission (email client to server) use?
questions.explanation: SMTP Submission uses port 587 (TCP) with STARTTLS. Port 25 is for server-to-server. Port 465 is for implicit TLS. Port 587 is the modern client submission standard.

questions.question: Which port does IMAPS (secure IMAP) use?
questions.explanation: IMAPS uses port 993 (TCP). It is IMAP encrypted with SSL/TLS for secure email access without changing email content on the server.

questions.question: Which port does POP3S (secure POP3) use?
questions.explanation: POP3S uses port 995 (TCP/UDP). It is POP3 encrypted with SSL/TLS for secure email downloading.

questions.question: Which port does FTPS (FTP over SSL) use for control?
questions.explanation: FTPS (implicit mode) uses port 990 (TCP) for control and port 989 for data. Explicit FTPS still uses port 21 but negotiates TLS with STARTTLS.

questions.question: Which port does SFTP use?
questions.explanation: SFTP (SSH File Transfer Protocol) uses port 22 — same as SSH. SFTP is a subsystem of SSH, not related to FTP despite the name similarity.
questions.takeaway: SFTP = Port 22 (SSH subsystem, not FTP over SSL)

questions.question: Which port does VPN protocol OpenVPN use?
questions.explanation: OpenVPN uses port 1194 (TCP/UDP). It is a popular open-source VPN. Can also run on port 443 to bypass firewalls.

questions.question: Which port does IPSec IKE (Internet Key Exchange) use?
questions.explanation: IPSec IKE uses port 500 (UDP). IKE negotiates security associations for IPSec. Port 4500 is used for NAT traversal.

questions.question: Which port does L2TP VPN use?
questions.explanation: L2TP (Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol) uses port 1701 (UDP). Usually combined with IPSec (L2TP/IPSec) for secure VPN connections.

questions.question: Which port does PPTP VPN use?
questions.explanation: PPTP (Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol) uses port 1723 (TCP). It is an older VPN protocol considered insecure due to known vulnerabilities.

questions.question: Which port does RADIUS use for authentication?
questions.explanation: RADIUS authentication uses port 1812 (TCP/UDP). The legacy port was 1645. RADIUS centralizes network access authentication.

questions.question: Which port does RADIUS Accounting use?
questions.explanation: RADIUS Accounting uses port 1813 (TCP/UDP). It records network usage data. Legacy accounting used port 1646.

questions.question: Which port does NFS use for network file sharing?
questions.explanation: NFS (Network File System) uses port 2049 (TCP/UDP). It enables file sharing between Unix/Linux systems over a network.

questions.question: Which port does Microsoft SQL Server use?
questions.explanation: Microsoft SQL Server uses port 1433 (TCP). Port 1434 is used by the SQL Server Browser service. This is commonly tested alongside MySQL (3306) and PostgreSQL (5432).
questions.takeaway: MS SQL Server = Port 1433 | MySQL = 3306 | PostgreSQL = 5432

questions.question: Which port does Oracle Database use?
questions.explanation: Oracle Database uses port 1521 (TCP) by default for the Oracle Listener. Ports 2483 and 2484 are used for Oracle's newer JDBC thin driver.

questions.question: Which port does Redis cache use?
questions.explanation: Redis uses port 6379 (TCP). Redis is an in-memory key-value store widely used for caching, session management, and pub/sub messaging.

questions.question: Which port does MongoDB NoSQL database use?
questions.explanation: MongoDB uses port 27017 (TCP/UDP). It is a popular document-oriented NoSQL database. Port 28017 was used for the legacy web interface.

questions.question: Which port does PostgreSQL use?
questions.explanation: PostgreSQL uses port 5432 (TCP). It is a powerful open-source relational database. Commonly tested alongside MySQL (3306) and MSSQL (1433).

questions.question: Which port does Elasticsearch use for REST API?
questions.explanation: Elasticsearch uses port 9200 (TCP/HTTP) for REST API queries. Port 9300 is used for inter-node cluster transport.

questions.question: Which port does Apache Cassandra use for CQL native protocol?
questions.explanation: Cassandra uses port 9042 (TCP) for CQL (Cassandra Query Language) connections. Port 7000 is used for inter-node communication.

questions.question: Which port does OSPF use for neighbor discovery?
questions.explanation: OSPF uses IP protocol number 89 directly — it does NOT use TCP or UDP ports. It communicates directly over IP using multicast addresses.

questions.question: Which port does IS-IS routing protocol use?
options: No port — uses ISO CLNP directly
questions.explanation: IS-IS (Intermediate System to Intermediate System) operates directly over Layer 2 (Data Link) using ISO CLNP — it does not use TCP, UDP, or IP port numbers.
questions.takeaway: IS-IS = Layer 2 protocol (no IP port, no TCP/UDP)

questions.question: Which port does VRRP use?
questions.explanation: VRRP (Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol) uses IP protocol number 112. It provides gateway redundancy without using TCP or UDP ports.

questions.question: Which port does Syslog use for sending log messages?
questions.explanation: Syslog uses port 514 (UDP). It is the standard for sending system event logs to a centralized log server. TLS syslog uses port 6514.

questions.question: Which port does SNMP Trap use to send alerts?
questions.explanation: SNMP Trap uses port 162 (UDP). Traps are alert messages sent by monitored devices to the SNMP manager — distinct from the agent query port 161.

questions.question: Which port does NetFlow (Cisco traffic monitoring) use?
questions.explanation: NetFlow uses port 2055 (UDP) by default. NetFlow exports traffic flow data from Cisco routers and switches to a collector for analysis.

questions.question: Which port does sFlow use for network traffic monitoring?
questions.explanation: sFlow (sampled flow) uses port 6343 (UDP). It is a multi-vendor standard for exporting traffic monitoring data, competing with Cisco's NetFlow.

questions.question: Which port does the Nessus vulnerability scanner use?
questions.explanation: Nessus uses port 1241 (TCP/UDP) for scanner communication. Nessus by Tenable is one of the most widely used vulnerability assessment tools.

questions.question: Which port does VNC remote desktop use?
questions.explanation: VNC (Virtual Network Computing) uses port 5900 (TCP). Port 5800 is used for VNC over HTTP (browser access). Compare with RDP on port 3389.

questions.question: Which port does Webmin system administration use?
questions.explanation: Webmin uses port 10000 (TCP). It provides a web-based interface for Unix/Linux system administration.

questions.question: Which port does Citrix ICA protocol use for virtual desktops?
questions.explanation: Citrix ICA (Independent Computing Architecture) uses port 1494 (TCP). It delivers virtual applications and desktops to thin clients.

questions.question: Which port does X11 (X Window System) use?
questions.explanation: X11 uses ports 6000-6001 (TCP). It provides graphical display capabilities for Unix/Linux systems over a network.

questions.question: Which port does XDMCP use for remote graphical login?
questions.explanation: XDMCP (X Display Manager Control Protocol) uses port 177 (UDP). It allows remote graphical login to X Window sessions.

questions.question: Which port does Docker daemon use for remote API (unencrypted)?
questions.explanation: Docker daemon uses port 2375 (TCP) for unencrypted remote API. Port 2376 is for TLS-encrypted API. Port 2377 is for Docker Swarm management.

questions.question: Which port does Kubernetes API server use?
questions.explanation: Kubernetes API server uses port 6443 (TCP). All kubectl commands and cluster communication go through this port.

questions.question: Which port does etcd (Kubernetes state store) use for clients?
questions.explanation: etcd uses port 2379 (TCP) for client communication and port 2380 for peer-to-peer replication between etcd nodes.

questions.question: Which port does Prometheus monitoring use?
questions.explanation: Prometheus uses port 9090 (TCP) for its HTTP API and web dashboard. It scrapes metrics from configured targets at regular intervals.

questions.question: Which port does Grafana dashboard use?
questions.explanation: Grafana uses port 3000 (TCP) for its web-based analytics and monitoring dashboard. It is commonly used with Prometheus.

questions.question: Which port does HashiCorp Vault secrets manager use?
questions.explanation: HashiCorp Vault uses port 8200 (TCP). It manages secrets, credentials, and certificates in DevOps environments.

questions.question: Which port does Jenkins CI/CD server use by default?
questions.explanation: Jenkins uses port 8080 (TCP) by default. It is one of the most widely used CI/CD automation servers.

questions.question: Which port does Apache Kafka broker use?
questions.explanation: Apache Kafka uses port 9092 (TCP) for producer/consumer connections. Port 9093 is used for SSL/TLS encrypted connections.

questions.question: Which port does RabbitMQ use for AMQP connections?
questions.explanation: RabbitMQ uses port 5672 (TCP) for AMQP connections. Port 5671 is for AMQP over TLS. Port 15672 is the management web UI.

questions.question: Which port does Apache ZooKeeper use for clients?
questions.explanation: ZooKeeper uses port 2181 (TCP) for client connections. It provides distributed coordination for applications like Kafka and Hadoop.

questions.question: Which port does SIP (VoIP signaling) use?
questions.explanation: SIP (Session Initiation Protocol) uses port 5060 (TCP/UDP) for VoIP call signaling. SIP-TLS uses port 5061 for encrypted signaling.

questions.question: Which port does RTP (Real-time Transport Protocol for VoIP media) use?
questions.explanation: RTP uses ports 5004-5005 (UDP) for carrying actual audio/video streams in VoIP. SIP on port 5060 controls the session; RTP carries the media.

questions.question: Which port does H.323 VoIP use for call signaling?
questions.explanation: H.323 uses port 1720 (TCP) for call signaling. H.323 is an older VoIP standard, largely replaced by SIP in modern deployments.

questions.question: Which port does RTSP (Real Time Streaming Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: RTSP uses port 554 (TCP/UDP). It controls multimedia streaming sessions — similar to a remote control for streams but doesn't carry the actual media.
questions.takeaway: RTSP = Port 554 (controls streams, not the media)

questions.question: Which port does TACACS+ use for AAA services?
questions.explanation: TACACS+ uses port 49 (TCP). It provides Authentication, Authorization, and Accounting (AAA) for network device access — Cisco's preferred AAA protocol.

questions.question: Which port does RADIUS use for authentication (modern standard)?
questions.explanation: Modern RADIUS authentication uses port 1812 (UDP). The legacy ports were 1645 (auth) and 1646 (accounting). RADIUS is IETF's standard AAA protocol.

questions.question: Which port does WINS (Windows Internet Name Service) use?
questions.explanation: WINS uses port 1512 (TCP/UDP). It resolves NetBIOS names to IP addresses in Windows environments — predecessor to DNS for Windows name resolution.

questions.question: Which port does HSRP (Cisco gateway redundancy) use?
questions.explanation: HSRP (Hot Standby Router Protocol) uses port 1985 (UDP). Cisco's HSRP provides gateway redundancy by sharing a virtual IP among multiple routers.

questions.question: Which port does GLBP (Cisco Gateway Load Balancing Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: GLBP (Gateway Load Balancing Protocol) uses port 3222 (TCP/UDP). It provides both gateway redundancy and load balancing — enhancement over HSRP and VRRP.

questions.question: Which port does LPD/LPR (Line Printer Daemon) use?
questions.explanation: LPD/LPR uses port 515 (TCP). It is the standard Unix/Linux printing protocol. IPP (port 631) is the modern replacement used by CUPS.

questions.question: Which port does IPP (Internet Printing Protocol / CUPS) use?
questions.explanation: IPP uses port 631 (TCP). It is the modern printing protocol used by CUPS on Linux/macOS — replacement for older LPD/LPR.

questions.question: Which port does AFP (Apple Filing Protocol) use?
questions.explanation: AFP uses port 548 (TCP). It is Apple's native file sharing protocol — used between Macs. SMB/CIFS (port 445) is now preferred even on macOS.

questions.question: Which port does iSCSI use for storage access?
questions.explanation: iSCSI uses port 3260 (TCP/UDP) for target connections. Port 860 is the legacy iSCSI port. iSCSI enables SCSI commands over TCP/IP networks.

questions.question: Which port does rsync use for file synchronization?
questions.explanation: rsync uses port 873 (TCP). It is an efficient file synchronization tool widely used for backups and mirroring on Unix/Linux.

questions.question: A student confuses IMAP (143) with IMAPS (993). What is the key difference?
options: IMAPS 993 downloads mail; IMAP 143 keeps on server
IMAP 143 uses UDP; IMAPS 993 uses TCP
IMAP 143 is unencrypted; IMAPS 993 uses SSL/TLS
questions.explanation: IMAP on port 143 is unencrypted. IMAPS on port 993 uses SSL/TLS encryption. The S suffix always means secure (encrypted). This is a common CCEE question.

questions.question: What is the difference between port 25 and port 587 for email?
options: Port 587 is unencrypted; Port 25 uses TLS
Port 25 is server-to-server SMTP; Port 587 is client-to-server email submission with STARTTLS
Port 25 is for POP3; Port 587 is for IMAP
questions.explanation: Port 25 is for server-to-server email relay. Port 587 is for email client submission (MUA to MTA) using STARTTLS. ISPs block port 25 outbound to prevent spam.

questions.question: What is the difference between FTP (20/21) and SFTP (22)?
options: SFTP uses ports 20 and 21 like FTP
SFTP is an SSH subsystem — completely different protocol from FTP
SFTP is FTP over SSL on port 22
FTP and SFTP use the same protocol
questions.explanation: Despite the name, SFTP is not FTP over SSL — it is an SSH subsystem (port 22) for file transfer. FTPS (ports 989/990) is actual FTP over SSL.

questions.question: What is the key difference between RDP (3389) and VNC (5900)?
options: RDP uses UDP; VNC uses TCP
RDP is Windows-native protocol; VNC is platform-independent pixel-based protocol
questions.explanation: RDP (port 3389) is Microsoft's proprietary protocol with high performance on Windows. VNC (port 5900) is platform-independent but sends raw screen pixels — slower.
questions.takeaway: RDP=3389 Windows native | VNC=5900 cross-platform pixel

questions.question: Which ports does Active Directory use? (Select the most complete answer)
questions.explanation: Active Directory uses many ports: 88 (Kerberos), 135 (RPC), 139/445 (SMB), 389 (LDAP), 636 (LDAPS), 3268/3269 (Global Catalog), 53 (DNS), 123 (NTP).

questions.question: Which port does mDNS use (Apple Bonjour / Avahi)?
questions.explanation: mDNS (Multicast DNS) uses port 5353 (UDP). Apple's Bonjour and Linux's Avahi use mDNS for zero-configuration service discovery on local networks.
